qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated NF instance registration permits rogue Network Function impersonation in free5gc NRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing authentication on NRF NF-management registration and discovery workflows, not the NRF Discovery EncodeGroupId malformed group-id-list parser path or its unchecked array indexing panic.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The target advisory is a denial-of-service in free5GC NRF Discovery where malformed group-id-list input reaches EncodeGroupId and triggers unchecked split-part indexing. The only manifest-listed qualifying finding instead describes a separate NRF authentication/authorization weakness.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7c47-xr7q-p6hg describes malformed group-id-list input causing EncodeGroupId to panic with index out of range in the NRF discovery service.

unauthenticated-nf-registration/vulnerability_report.json reports unauthenticated PUT /nnrf-nfm/v1/nf-instances/{nfInstanceID} and default OAuth behavior, with no evidence for EncodeGroupId, group-id-list handling, or unchecked array indexing.
Repository-session search found no qualifying finding evidence naming EncodeGroupId or the advisory group-id-list panic path.
